title: Vulnerability Age Tracking
spl:
index=security tag=vulnerability status=open
| eval age_days=(now()-strptime(first_found,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400
| where age_days>90
| stats sum(age_days) as total_age by dest, cve
description: Time open since `first_found` drives SLA and board reporting.
value: Time open since `first_found` drives SLA and board reporting.
implementation: Join CVSS for prioritization; exclude accepted risks with GRC ID.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` latest(Vulnerabilities.first_found) as ff from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.cve
| eval age_days=(now()-ff)/86400
| where age_days>90
knownFalsePositives: Vulnerability aging and risk noise when CMDB sync lags, accepted risks are not in the report, or scan windows miss maintenance clusters; de-duplicate by asset and exception.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Tenable, Qualys → Vulnerabilities.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `Vulnerabilities` CIM with `status`, `cve`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Join CVSS for prioritization; exclude accepted risks with GRC ID.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=vulnerability status=open
| eval age_days=(now()-strptime(first_found,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400
| where age_days>90
| stats sum(age_days) as total_age by dest, cve
```

Understanding this SPL

**Vulnerability Age Tracking** — Time open since `first_found` drives SLA and board reporting.

Documented **Data sources**: `Vulnerabilities` CIM with `status`, `cve`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Tenable, Qualys → Vulnerabilities. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>90` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by dest, cve** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` latest(Vulnerabilities.first_found) as ff from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.cve
| eval age_days=(now()-ff)/86400
| where age_days>90
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Vulnerability Age Tracking** — Time open since `first_found` drives SLA and board reporting.

Documented **Data sources**: `Vulnerabilities` CIM with `status`, `cve`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Tenable, Qualys → Vulnerabilities. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>90` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Histogram (age), Table (oldest 20).
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "Vulnerability Age Tracking" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
